input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule S.3.12.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: s.3.12.4
            TITLE: penalty method a - fixed point deductions

            CONTENT:
            rule S.3.12.4: Penalty Method A - Fixed Point Deductions
S.3.12.4 Penalty Method A - Fixed Point Deductions 
a. From the Bill of Material, the Cost Judges will determine if all Parts and Processes have 
been included in the analysis.    
b. In the case of any omission or error a penalty proportional to the BOM level of the error 
will be imposed:  
• Missing/inaccurate Material, Process, Fastener  1 point 
• Missing/inaccurate Part     3 point 
• Missing/inaccurate Assembly    5 point 
c. Each of the penalties listed above supersedes the previous penalty.   
Example - if a point deduction is given for a missing Assembly, the missing Parts are ignored.
Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International  Page 122 of 140   
Version 1.0   1 Sep 2023 
d. Differences other than those listed above will be deducted at the discretion of the Cost 
Judges.

final_answer: RULE: s.3.12.4 TITLE: penalty method a - fixed point deductions CONTENT: rule S.3.12.4: Penalty Method A - Fixed Point Deductions S.3.12.4 Penalty Method A - Fixed Point Deductions a. From the Bill of Material, the Cost Judges will determine if all Parts and Processes have been included in the analysis. b. In the case of any omission or error a penalty proportional to the BOM level of the error will be imposed: • Missing/inaccurate Material, Process, Fastener 1 point • Missing/inaccurate Part 3 point • Missing/inaccurate Assembly 5 point c. Each of the penalties listed above supersedes the previous penalty. Example - if a point deduction is given for a missing Assembly, the missing Parts are ignored. Formula SAE® Rules 2024 © 2023 SAE International Page 122 of 140 Version 1.0 1 Sep 2023 d. Differences other than those listed above will be deducted at the discretion of the Cost Judges.
